Bone Hatchet.description: A small hatchet with a bone-inlaid handle, quickly thrown or drawn in close quarters

Frost Blade.description: A blade tempered in permafrost. Its edge holds a piercing cold that numbs on contact.

Shaman's Totem Staff.description: A totem staff carved with taiga spirits. Greatly amplifies elemental spells.

Permafrost Spear.description: A long spear with a tip carved from solid permafrost. Keeps its edge in any cold.

Snowbinable Fang Sword.description: A sword forged from the legendary fang of the Snowbinable. Radiates an aura of deep permafrost.

Siberian Wolf Fang.description: A large fang from a Siberian wolf, valued for its sharpness.

Bear Claw.description: A sturdy claw from a brown bear. Useful in crafting or trade.

Bear Hide.description: A thick brown bear hide, excellent for crafting warm armor.

Lynx Pelt.description: A soft spotted pelt from a taiga lynx. Prized by furriers.

Permafrost Shard.description: A chunk of ancient frozen ground from deep in the taiga. Cold to the touch.

Taiga Herb.description: A medicinal herb found growing under the snow. Restores 35 HP.

Shaman's Bone Totem.description: A small carved bone totem imbued with frost shaman magic.

Wolverine Hide.description: A dense, water-repellent hide from a ferocious wolverine. Highly durable.

Snow Leopard Pelt.description: An exquisite pelt from a snow leopard. Worth a great deal to merchants.

Permafrost Core.description: The frozen heart of a permafrost golem. Still radiates intense cold.

Spirit Essence.description: A crystallized drop of taiga spirit energy. Shimmers with faint green light.

Snowbinable Heart.description: The legendary frozen heart of the Snowbinable. Pulsates with ancient permafrost energy.

Ottoman Scimitar.description: A elegantly curved blade forged in the Ottoman tradition. Light and brutally sharp.

Yatagan.description: A short Ottoman sword with an inward-curved blade, favoured by the Janissaries.

Ottoman Bow.description: A powerful recurve bow used by Ottoman cavalry and desert raiders alike.

Ottoman Lance.description: A long cavalry lance used in Ottoman charges. Devastating on the first strike.

Corsair Cutlass.description: A curved sabre used by Ottoman corsairs. The blade is notched from countless sea battles.

Desert Serpent Blade.description: A sinuous blade shaped like a coiled serpent. Its tip is coated in desert venom.

Vizier Staff.description: The ornate staff of an Ottoman court mage, set with sapphires and gilded in gold leaf.

Janissary Carbine.description: An early firearm used by elite Janissary troops. Loud, dangerous, and slow to reload.

Sultan's Blade.description: The ceremonial sword of the Sultan. Its golden hilt is encrusted with rubies and its edge never dulls.

Blade of Yildirim.description: Sultan Yildirim's personal war sword, said to have tasted a thousand victories. Radiates commanding energy.

Ottoman Chainmail.description: Finely-linked chainmail forged by Ottoman armorers. Flexible yet strong.

Corsair Coat.description: A thick leather coat worn by Ottoman sea pirates. Reinforced at the shoulders and chest.

Janissary Plate Armor.description: Heavy plate armor worn by the Ottoman Empire's elite Janissary corps. Imposing and near-impenetrable.

Vizier's Silken Robes.description: Luxurious robes of the Grand Vizier, threaded with protective enchantments woven by court mages.

Sultan's Gilded Plate.description: Ceremonial plate armor of the Sultan himself, covered in gold filigree and empire symbols.

Sultan's Signet Ring.description: A heavy gold ring bearing the seal of the Ottoman Sultan. Commands respect — and fear.

Corsair's Amulet.description: A sea-stone amulet worn by Ottoman pirates for luck on dangerous waters.

Janissary Medallion.description: A brass medallion awarded to elite Janissary soldiers. Increases combat focus.

Ottoman Turban of Wisdom.description: An elaborately wrapped turban imbued with the wisdom of Ottoman scholars.

Ottoman Gold Coin.description: A gold coin bearing the Sultan's tughra. Valuable anywhere in the world.

Spice Bundle.description: A bundle of rare spices from the eastern trade routes. Worth a handsome sum.

Janissary Badge.description: The insignia badge of a Janissary soldier — proof of a hard-won fight.

Desert Jewel.description: A gemstone mined from the Anatolian plains, prized for its deep amber colour.

Corsair Flag.description: The battle flag of an Ottoman corsair ship. Collectors pay well for these.

Slave Chain.description: A rusted chain from Port Kostantora's slave market. A grim reminder of the cost of empire.

Lion's Mane Trophy.description: The magnificent mane of an Anatolian lion. A prized hunting trophy.

Desert Serpent Fang.description: The hollow venom fang of a desert serpent. Used in exotic poison-craft.

Elephant Tusk.description: An ivory tusk from an Ottoman war elephant. Immensely valuable.

Sultan's Seal.description: The personal wax seal of Sultan Yildirim. An extremely rare prize.

Grand Vizier's Grimoire.description: The spell-tome of Grand Vizier Karamanli, filled with forbidden Ottoman court magic.

Ottoman Silk.description: Exquisite silk from Ottoman looms. Merchants will pay a fortune.

The Fall of Constanplea.description: A survivor's account of the siege and fall of Constanplea. Written by Scholar Theophanes in exile.

The New Order: Life in Kostantora.description: A personal account of Kostantora fifteen years after the conquest — the slave markets, faith, and survival. By Miriam of the Lower City.

Chronicles of Byzantra.description: A concise history of the Byzantra Empire from founding to fall. Compiled by the Byzantra Academy of History.

Origins of the Kalovar People.description: A scholarly history tracing the Kalovar people from the steppes north of Yixuan to empire. By Archivist Palaiologos.

The Faith and Law of the Kalovanim Empir.description: An official Kalovar guide for travellers. Informative, one-sided, and useful in its own way.

The Origin of Eldenmoor.description: A scholarly transcription of the Primordial Scriptures — texts said to record how Andrew, the Primordial One, created the world using sacred commands no mortal has ever understood. Cheap because everyone assumes it's fiction.

The Warrior's Code of Byzantra.description: A field manual and philosophical treatise on warfare by General Andronikos Doukas. Practical and unexpectedly moving.

Voices from the Steppes.description: A collection of Kalovar oral poems and tales gathered by Scholar Erasimos. Strange and illuminating.

The Merchant's Road.description: A practical traveller's guide to trade routes and city markets across the known world. Annotated extensively.

The Last Basileus.description: A biography of Konstantinos IX, last emperor of Byzantra. Written in exile by Lady Eudokia Asanina.

Talisman of Andy.description: A sacred talisman forged in the heart of the Eternal Flame, blessed by ancient spirits. Wielded only by those chosen by destiny itself. Gifted freely on the day of its creator’s birth.

The River Kingdoms of Yixuan.description: A geographic survey of the eastern continent and its river kingdoms, by Scholar Periandros.

Tales of the Jade Court.description: Stories and parables from the eastern Jade Court. Clever, strange, and illuminating.

Songs of the Northern Clans.description: Barbarian sagas and hearth songs of the Varak Confederation, collected by a border scholar.

The Iron Horde.description: A military history of the northern incursions, told from both sides of the border.

The Eastern Alchemist's Compendium.description: Yixuan medicines and healing philosophy, translated by a sceptical western physician who became less sceptical.

Origia: A History of the First Settlemen.description: A Bureau of Historical Records account of the village most call Starting Village — its true name, its founding, and why the world keeps forgetting what it is actually called.

Waymarks and Namings.description: The Cartographers' Guild's official lexicon of western place names, with corrected entries for Origia, Ashvale, Thornwick, and Eldenmoor.

Yixuan Silk.description: Lustrous silk from the eastern kingdoms, lighter than linen and stronger than you'd expect

Bamboo Fiber.description: Stripped and cured bamboo fibre, surprisingly tough and flexible

Jade Fragment.description: A chip of genuine eastern jade, prized for both its beauty and its alleged protective properties

Eastern Spice Bundle.description: A wrapped bundle of black pepper, star anise, and dried ginger from Yixuan markets

Barbarian Iron Chunk.description: Crudely smelted iron from the northern clans — impure, but their smiths know tricks with it

Clan War-Paint Pigment.description: Thick pigment used by the Varak clans in battle rituals. Said to carry old blessing

Lotus Root.description: The starchy root of the jade lotus plant, used in eastern medicines and cooking

Rice Bundle.description: A tied sheaf of harvested rice, ready for milling or trade

Fermented Soybean Paste.description: A pungent eastern ingredient made from aged soybeans. Essential for many Yixuan preparations

Northern Fur Pelt.description: A thick pelt from the northern wilderness, warm enough for any winter

Barbarian Totem Shard.description: A broken fragment of a clan totem pole, carved with northern spirit-marks

Dao Blade.description: A gracefully curved eastern sword with a single cutting edge, fast and precise

Jade Spear.description: A long thrusting spear tipped with hardened jade, its shaft wrapped in silk cord

Bamboo Staff.description: A seasoned bamboo staff reinforced with iron bands — light, fast, and deceptively powerful

Serpent Fang Dagger.description: A curved dagger shaped like a serpent's fang, favoured by Yixuan river-clan assassins

Silk-Wrapped Yari.description: An elegant eastern polearm with a cross-guard, its grip wrapped in layered silk

Jade Dragon Sword.description: A masterwork blade from the forges of River's Crown, with a jade-inlaid dragon hilt. Legendary in the east.

Clan Greataxe.description: A massive double-headed axe from the northern clans — brutal, heavy, and extraordinarily effective

Warbrand.description: A long, heavy two-handed sword used by Varak clan champions — massive reach, devastating power

Skull-Crusher Maul.description: An iron-shod wooden war-maul. Not elegant. Very effective.

Iron Horde Axe.description: A ceremonial war-axe of the Iron Horde confederation, marked with clan-binding runes

Bear-Claw Gauntlet.description: Iron gauntlets ending in sharpened bear-claw blades, worn by the Varak Bear Warrior order

Yixuan Silk Robe.description: A flowing robe of finest eastern silk, light as air yet resistant to blades. Favoured by eastern scholars and mages.

Lacquered Eastern Plate.description: Overlapping lacquered iron plates in the Yixuan style, lighter than western plate and equally protective

Barbarian War-Paint Leather.description: Heavy northern leather daubed with clan war-paint. The pigment carries old blessings that actually seem to help.

Fur-Lined Clan Cloak.description: A thick northern cloak lined with layered furs, worn by Varak clan warriors through brutal winters

Jade-Threaded Vest.description: A lightly armoured eastern vest with jade beads woven through the fabric for protection and focus

Jade Tea.description: A calming eastern tea brewed from chrysanthemum and wolfberry — restores MP and steadies the mind

Eastern Herb Medicine.description: A dark paste of eastern medicinal herbs, bitter to taste and remarkably effective

Warrior's Brew.description: A Varak clan drink brewed from fermented barley and northern roots. Tastes terrible. Works.

Jade Lotus Draught.description: A distillation of jade lotus petals with profound restorative properties. Rare and revered in Yixuan.

Pepper Tonic.description: A concentrated dose of eastern black pepper in tonic water — improves circulation and speed

Kalovar Sabre.description: A curved cavalry sabre favoured by Kalovar mounted soldiers — fast, single-edged, and lethal at range

Corsair's Cutlass.description: A heavy-bladed seafarer's sword, nicked from years of use but still razor sharp where it counts

Kalovar War Lance.description: A long cavalry lance used by Kalovar heavy horse — devastating on the charge, unwieldy on foot

Steppe Recurve Bow.description: A short but powerful recurve bow made from layered bone and sinew — the Varak steppe archer's weapon of choice

Bear Warrior Axe.description: A specially weighted axe used by initiates of the Varak Bear Warrior order, bearing the First Mother's mark

Yixuan River Blade.description: A thin, flexible sword forged in the workshops of River's Crown from Yixuan river-stone and folded steel

Jade Court Rapier.description: A slim and elegant thrusting sword used by Jade Court Assassins. Almost too beautiful to be so deadly.

Constanplea Ghost Blade.description: An ancient sword excavated from the Constanplea ruins, still bearing a faint blue light. The dead recognise it.

Kalovar Grand Vizier's Staff.description: The ceremonial war-staff of a Kalovar Grand Vizier, crackling with court magic and political authority

Iron Horde Standard.description: A clan battle standard of the Iron Horde. Inspires Varak warriors to near-supernatural ferocity — and terrifies everyone else.

Varak Totem Shield.description: A large round shield faced with carved clan totems. Heavy as stone, tough as the northern winter.

Primordial Birthday Cake.description: A towering, multi-layered cake baked by the cosmos itself in honour of Andrew — The Primordial One's birthday. The frosting shimmers with stardust, the candles burn with the light of newborn stars, and each slice tastes of every good thing ever created. It fully restores your health and mana, fills you with a profound sense of celebratory joy, and grants a generous helping of experience — because on Andrew's birthday, everyone levels up a little.

Primordial Essence.description: A crystallized fragment of the cosmos itself, bestowed by a being older than time. Its inner light pulses with the weight of countless ages. Consuming it floods your very soul with an incomprehensible surge of experience — the lifetime of a god compressed into a single breath. Extremely rare and impossibly expensive.

Grand Feast Platter.description: A divine spread of roasted meats, enchanted fruits, celestial bread, and bottles of restorative ambrosia, prepared in the feasting halls of the gods and delivered by winged messengers. Eating it fully restores your health and mana, and leaves you with a vigorous glow that keeps regenerating both for a time.
